Class Input

java.lang.Object
input.Input
public class Input extends Object
The Input class acts as a global manager for keyboard and mouse states. * It stores and manages the lifecycle of input events, differentiating between continuous states (held down) and discrete events (just pressed/released). This design prevents multiple triggers for a single action and ensures synchronized input handling across the game update cycle.

  • Constructor Details

    • Input

      public Input()

  • Method Details

    • onKeyPressed

      public static void onKeyPressed(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent e)
      Processes key press events and updates internal state buffers. Marks a key as "pressed" only if it was not already being held in the previous frame.
      Parameters:
      e - The KeyEvent captured from the JavaFX scene

    • onKeyReleased

      public static void onKeyReleased(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent e)
      Processes key release events and removes them from the active set.
      Parameters:
      e - The KeyEvent captured from the JavaFX scene

    • isPressed

      public static boolean isPressed(javafx.scene.input.KeyCode key)
      Checks if a specific key was pressed during the current frame.
      Parameters:
      key - The KeyCode to check
      Returns:
      true if the key transition to pressed occurred this frame

    • update

      public static void update()
      Clears the discrete event buffers (pressed/released states). This should be called at the end of every frame to reset transient input states.

    • setMousePosition

      public static void setMousePosition(double x, double y)
      Updates the global mouse coordinates.
      Parameters:
      x - The horizontal coordinate in the game window
      y - The vertical coordinate in the game window

    • getMouseX

      public static double getMouseX()
      Returns:
      The current X-coordinate of the mouse.

    • getMouseY

      public static double getMouseY()
      Returns:
      The current Y-coordinate of the mouse.

    • reset

      public static void reset()
      Resets all key buffers, typically used when changing scenes or focus.

    • onMousePressed

      public static void onMousePressed(javafx.scene.input.MouseButton button)
      Processes mouse button press events. Tracks both primary (left) and secondary (right) clicks while identifying the initial frame of the click.
      Parameters:
      button - The MouseButton type that was pressed

    • onMouseReleased

      public static void onMouseReleased(javafx.scene.input.MouseButton button)
      Processes mouse button release events and resets button state.
      Parameters:
      button - The MouseButton type that was released

    • isLeftClick

      public static boolean isLeftClick()
      Returns:
      true if the left mouse button was clicked in the current frame.

    • isRightClick

      public static boolean isRightClick()
      Returns:
      true if the right mouse button was clicked in the current frame.
